title: Campylostachys cernua
taxonomy: Campylostachys cernua is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. This plant is native to South Africa and is a member of the genus Campylostachys. The classification of Campylostachys cernua is further specified as a species of bulbous perennial, which is a common characteristic of many plants within the Amaryllidaceae family. The classification of this plant is also supported by its unique characteristics, such as its strap-shaped leaves and funnel-shaped flowers. Campylostachys cernua is considered to be a relatively rare and lesser-known species within the Amaryllidaceae family.

intro: Campylostachys cernua, also known as Strobilanthes cernua or Blue Pencilled Plant, is a plant species in the family Acanthaceae, characterized by its vibrant blue flowers and unique foliage. Traditionally, Campylostachys cernua has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
